title: danthonia involucrata
intro: Danthonia involucrata, also known as Poverty Grass or Poverty Oat Grass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The medicinal properties of Danthonia involucrata include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is also said to have health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Traditionally, Danthonia involucrata is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
